name: Feature request

labels: enhancement

## The problem

<!-- What can't you do today? Concrete use case, not a solution. -->

## Proposed direction

<!-- What you think should change. If it's new conformance coverage, name the
     RFC-1 section(s) it would exercise. -->

## Spec grounding

<!-- muster's behavior is defined by Soul.md RFC-1. Which section(s) does this
     relate to? If the proposal goes beyond RFC-1 (e.g. a new spec adapter, an
     extension namespace), say so explicitly — the spec-agnostic core is meant
     to allow that, but it's a deliberate scope decision. -->

## Scope check

Please confirm you've considered:

- [ ] This stays within RFC-1, **or** it's an explicit new-spec/extension
  proposal (which belongs behind the adapter interface, not in the core).
- [ ] It doesn't require a new runtime dependency (or you've noted why one is
  unavoidable — see `CONTRIBUTING.md` §"Minimal dependencies").
- [ ] It doesn't reopen a decision already locked in a `kitty-specs/` mission
  spec or mission-review (and if it does, you've explained why the prior
  reasoning no longer holds).

<!-- What else could solve the same problem, and why this is better. -->
